mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.09% suggests accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during easing periods not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 5.8% provides baseline for deterioration detection
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS and HOUST signals robust consumer demand and housing activity that sustains corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to show month-over-month growth simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 449282M and HOUST at 1009K units
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in < 52% of periods when both RSAFS and HOUST accelerate simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO exceeding 1.5% over a 3-month period signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity investors reprrice earnings and inflation expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between INDPRO momentum and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in PAYEMS over a 6-month horizon combined with stable or declining UNRATE signals tight labor market conditions that support consumer income and spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 140045K persons, UNRATE at 5.8%
regime_hint: tight labor market with sustained employment growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher when both conditions hold versus when only one holds (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, the monetary policy regime shifts to restrictive stance, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This mechanism is regime-conditional and inactive when FEDFUNDS is below 4.5%.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.09% indicates mechanism is inactive at decision point
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 during high-rate regimes over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in CPIAUCSL growth rate (month-over-month) below 0.2% combined with rising RSAFS signals disinflation and robust real consumer demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity risk premiums compress.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with disinflation and demand recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in < 53% of periods when both disinflation and RSAFS growth are present simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 2-quarter decline in GDPC1 growth rate signals economic contraction and earnings compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reprice recession risk.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate declines > 0.5pp quarter-over-quarter for 2 consecutive quarters
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window during contraction periods.
